title: OCRRA Battery Recycling Kiosk Locations Debated: Youth Center Safety and Staffing Concerns
summary: Continued debate from Jan 7 study session on OCRRA battery recycling kiosk locations at community centers. Council members raise concerns about placing kiosks at youth-focused centers with limited staff (2 per site). Alternatives suggested include public libraries, City Hall, and Bob Cecil Center (adult-focused). DOE grant funding tied to city placement. Items 14-15 withdrawn. Item 13 held for committee meeting.

political_signal.description: Council holds OCRRA battery kiosk agreement citing safety and staffing concerns at youth-focused community centers

processing_notes: Fire Department detection contextual — battery fire hazards discussed, not presenting dept
Councilor Ehrenreich's first meeting — states 'I believe I have the votes' to carry item
Locations debated: Wilson Park, West Morland, Kirk Park (youth-focused, too small) vs Bob Cecil Center (adult-focused, acceptable)
Segment 4 of 12 from Common Council Study Session, Monday, January 12th 2026
